(3) The Lagna at conception may be the central point between
the Lagna and the Moon at birth.
I have come across another method for arriving at the Niseka-
time from the birth-data : Note the exact positions of the Sun,
Moon and ascendant at birth. Now subtract the ascendant

ISSUE 179
from the Moon and convert the result into degrees. Keep
this in two places. Divide one of these figures by 16 and add
the quotient to the figure kept apart. Divide this result by 14.
To the quotient thus got add the constant Ahargana of 246.
This will give the number of days preceding the birth
i.
